Q1 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the Entity-Relationship (ER) model, what is the cardinality ratio that represents a relationship where each entity instance in one entity set is associated with exactly one entity instance in another entity set?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
In a 1 : 1 relationship, each entity instance in one entity set is associated with exactly one entity instance in another entity set.

Q3 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the relational model, what does normalization refer to?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Normalization in the relational model involves organizing data to reduce redundancy and
dependency, thereby improving data integrity.

Correct answer: D

(d)

Q7 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true from the statement about normal forms?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
(a) Lossless join and dependency preserving decomposition into 3NF is always possible. True
(b) Lossless join and dependency preserving decomposition into BCNF may or may not possible.
False
(c) Two attribute relation like R(A, B) satisfied are normal form: 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, BCNF, 4NF, 5NF, 6NF. True
(d) BCNF is stronger than 3NF. True

Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
S1 : Selection and projection is not commutative.
S2 : A consecutive relation condition can be broken up into a cascade of individual relation operation.
So, S2 is true.

(b)
